input.prompt: Your team is upgrading the codebase from .NET 9 to .NET 10. You have been asked to benchmark AverageWordLength, WordLengthHistogram, and UniqueWordCount to check how the upgrade will impact their performance. The source code is in the TextAnalyzer subdirectory (TextAnalyzer.cs and TextAnalyzer.csproj). We don't need anything too comprehensive, don't spend more than 10 minutes on this. Don't worry about cleaning up any files or projects you create along the way. Note: this machine may be under load from other processes. If the .NET 9 runtime is missing, install it using the official dotnet-install script from https://dot.net/v1/dotnet-install.sh (or dotnet-install.ps1 on Windows).

data.reasoningText:
The BenchmarkDotNet version doesn't have Net10_0 yet. Let me check what version is installed and what monikers are available. I could try a newer version of BenchmarkDotNet, or use a nightly build. Or I could use a custom job configuration.

Let me try a different approach: use `Job.Default.WithRuntime(CoreRuntime.CreateForNewVersion("net10.0", ".NET 10.0"))`.

data.content: BenchmarkDotNet 0.14 doesn't support .NET 10. Let me write a manual benchmark harness instead — it'll be quicker and reliable.
data.reasoningText:
BenchmarkDotNet 0.14.0 doesn't support .NET 10 properly. I have a few options:

1. Try a newer/nightly version of BenchmarkDotNet
2. Write a manual benchmark instead

Let me try a newer version of BenchmarkDotNet first. The latest might have net10 support. Let me check if there's a 0.15.x or a preview version.

Actually, let me just write a simple manual benchmark. It'll be faster and more reliable than trying to find a compatible BenchmarkDotNet version. BenchmarkDotNet is quite picky about runtime versions.


I'll build a console app that runs warmup iterations followed by timed runs for each method, and I can target both net9.0 and net10.0 separately to compile and execute them independently.

toolCalls.name: edit
toolCalls.args:
{"new_str": "<Project Sdk="Microsoft.NET.Sdk">
  <PropertyGroup>
    <OutputType>Exe</OutputType>
    <TargetFrameworks>net9.0;net10.0</TargetFrameworks>
    <ImplicitUsings>enable</ImplicitUsings>
    <Nullable>enable</Nullable>
  </PropertyGroup>
  <ItemGroup>
    <Compile Include="../TextAnalyzer/TextAnalyzer.cs" Link="TextAnalyzer.cs" />
  </ItemGroup>
</Project>
", "old_str": "<Project Sdk="Microsoft.NET.Sdk">
  <PropertyGroup>
    <OutputType>Exe</OutputType>
    <TargetFrameworks>net9.0;net10.0</TargetFrameworks>
    <ImplicitUsings>enable</ImplicitUsings>
    <Nullable>enable</Nullable>
  </PropertyGroup>
  <ItemGroup>
    <PackageReference Include="BenchmarkDotNet" Version="0.14.*" />
  </ItemGroup>
  <ItemGroup>
    <Compile Include="../TextAnalyzer/TextAnalyzer.cs" Link="TextAnalyzer.cs" />
  </ItemGroup>
</Project>
", "path": "/tmp/sv-0c0836cb6ac348d69d150fd7d62a8851/Benchmarks/Benchmarks.csproj"}
